matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: 15q11.1-q11.2 copy-number region including POTEB3 (Benign ClinVar regional CNV context)
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a POTEB3/POTE-family structural-region thesis: the individual may carry a signal in a highly paralogous 15q11.2 cancer-testis gene region where assembly choice, copy number, and read-mapping uniqueness matter more than single-SNV heuristics. This is a paralog-aware research flag, not a POTEB3-specific diagnosis or methylation prediction.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: Observed 15q11.1-q11.2 copy-number context including POTEB3 suggests a regional structural-variation thesis: the sample matched a broad segmental-duplication CNV context that includes POTEB3 among many genes. Keep this interpretation regional and assembly-aware; it is not a POTEB3-specific pathogenic or protective call.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched 15q11.1-q11.2 copy-number region including POTEB3 at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the POTEB3 prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Large regional copy-number event rather than a single POTEB3 coding or regulatory variant.

evidence.label: ClinVar RCV000136295: benign copy-number loss spanning POTEB3

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a POTEB3/POTE-family structural-region thesis: the individual may carry a signal in a highly paralogous 15q11.2 cancer-testis gene region where assembly choice, copy number, and read-mapping uniqueness matter more than single-SNV heuristics. This is a paralog-aware research flag, not a POTEB3-specific diagnosis or methylation prediction. An observed POTEB3 variant places this sample in the curated POTEB3 research context. The local POTEB3 knowledge base is paralog-aware and copy-number-context oriented. POTEB3 lies in the complex 15q11.2 POTE segmental-duplication region, has no direct GRCh37 primary-locus projection from Ensembl mapping, and should not be interpreted like a high-confidence single-copy clinical gene. Most POTEB3 findings should be treated as structural-variation, segmental-duplication, expression, or POTE-family research context rather than as single-nucleotide diagnostic calls.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
